questions.question: According to surveys of USAF members, which foundational competency has been consistently rated as the most important for success in a USAF career?
Accountability is defined as demonstrating reliability and honesty and taking responsibility for the behaviors of whom?
What term refers to a person's belief in their ability to address a situation, based on the skills they have and the circumstances they face?
Which mindset refers to the belief that a person's intelligence and personal qualities are mostly permanent and cannot be significantly changed?
People with a growth mindset are likely to do which of the following when they encounter challenges?
options: Give up easily because the task is too difficult
View exerting effort as evidence of inability
Persist on even the most difficult tasks and focus on improving
Become defensive about mistakes and negative feedback

questions.question: According to the text, self-control is compared to what physical attribute?
options: A battery that drains and recharges
A muscle that gets depleted with heavy use but can be strengthened with practice
A reservoir that empties and refills over time
A circuit breaker that trips under overload

questions.question: According to the text on self-control, what strategy is more likely to prevent inappropriate behavior than simply trying to internally control urges?
options: Practicing meditation and deep breathing exercises
Setting daily written reminders of personal goals
Changing the situation to reduce temptation
Seeking counseling from a mental health professional

questions.question: Studies have found that even how many weeks of deliberate, daily practice to inhibit an urge or behavior can meaningfully improve impulse control?
What does positive reappraisal refer to in the context of resilience?
options: Ignoring negative events and focusing only on positive outcomes
The process of re-interpreting stressful events as non-threatening, meaningful, and even contributing to personal growth
Replacing negative thoughts with positive affirmations daily
Seeking external validation to overcome stressful experiences

questions.question: When a person frames a stressful situation in terms of challenge rather than threat, what physiological outcome is observed?
options: Heart rate, pulse, and blood pressure do not increase at all
The person experiences no anxiety about the task
Heart rate, pulse, and blood pressure return to normal more quickly after the event
Stress hormones are completely eliminated during the event

questions.question: What are the five core principles of effective communication represented by the acronym FOCUS?
According to the text, what is the most important aspect of good communication?
What is the seventh and final step for effective communication as indicated in DAFH 33-337?
options: Fight for feedback and get approval

questions.question: According to the text, the purpose of all job interviews is designed with what single goal in mind?
options: To evaluate a candidate's technical knowledge
To find the right person for the right job
To assess communication and leadership skills
To determine salary and benefit requirements

questions.question: Which USAF paper type is used when addressing a single issue that can be covered within a single page using bullets or phrases that require minimal data, and functions as a memory jogger?
What are the three common delivery formats for spoken communication?
According to the text, a superb impromptu speaker has achieved what level in verbal communications?
What are the three qualities that every good briefing must have?
According to the text, communication experts believe that over half of the meaning of any message may be communicated in what way?
What is the normal spoken rate referenced in the text for verbal communication?
What nonverbal quirk term is used when a speaker paces across a stage from one side to the other without stopping?
What is the difference between hearing and listening as described in the text?
options: Hearing is involuntary while listening is a trained military skill
Hearing occurs when ears pick up sounds; listening involves hearing while also paying attention to and giving consideration to what is heard
Hearing requires focus while listening is passive reception of sound
There is no functional difference; the terms are interchangeable

questions.question: In e-mail etiquette, writing in all CAPITAL letters is considered the equivalent of what?
According to the text, what is the Department of Defense's core messaging system of record?
According to the organizational communication section, what five characteristics must an effective organizational culture exhibit to foster a culture of engagement?
options: Transparent, honest, accountable, responsive, and resilient
Proactive, innovative, adaptive, leader driven, and sustainable
Structured, disciplined, mission-focused, team-oriented, and agile
Inclusive, diverse, flexible, results-driven, and collaborative
